name: Hri Kumar Nair
current_positions: Justice of the Court of Appeal, Supreme Court of Singapore

work_experience.position: Member of Parliament (Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC, Thomson division)
Deputy Attorney-General of Singapore (first ex-lawmaker to take on public prosecutor role)
Justice of the Court of Appeal

person_relationships.description: Menon is Chief Justice and President of the Court of Appeal; Nair serves as Justice of the Court of Appeal under Menon
Both served as MPs for Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC together (2006-2015)
Represented the government against Tan's constitutional challenge to the elected presidency in 2017

family_members.industry_or_organization: Partner at Ernst & Young; diagnosed with lymphoma in 2012, which led Nair to retire from politics

political_stances.stance_content: Former member of the People's Action Party (PAP); served as MP from 2006-2015 before transitioning to judicial service

Proposed during Parliamentary debate that government should appoint Cabinet Ministers from Nominated MPs to draw on experience of capable Singaporeans; critics said this would deepen parochial political culture and reduce accountability
Spoke out in favour of repealing Section 377A of the Penal Code, which criminalised homosexual sex in Singapore
Conducted a prosecutorial review for the wrongful conviction case of domestic worker Parti Liyani, demonstrating commitment to judicial fairness

major_achievements.achievement: Appointed Deputy Attorney-General of Singapore; first former lawmaker to take on the role of public prosecutor
Handled high-profile cases including the City Harvest Church trial appeal and Tan Cheng Bock's constitutional challenge to the elected presidency
Successfully argued the prosecution's appeal for the death penalty in the Chia Kee Chen murder case
Led prosecution team in the 'Gardens by the Bay murder' case (Leslie Khoo, convicted of murdering Cui Yajie)
Conducted prosecutorial review in the Parti Liyani wrongful conviction case
Appointed Judge of the High Court by President Halimah Yacob
Appointed Justice of the Court of Appeal by President Tharman Shanmugaratnam

biography_summary: Hri Kumar Nair SC (born 16 June 1966) is a Singaporean lawyer and former politician who has served as a Justice of the Court of Appeal since October 2025. The youngest of nine children, he graduated from the National University of Singapore with a Second Class Honours (Upper) in Law in 1991 and was called to the bar in 1992. He began his career at Drew & Napier LLC, where he spent over 25 years as a litigator and was appointed Senior Counsel in 2008. A former member of the People's Action Party, he served as MP for Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC (Thomson division) from 2006 to 2015, retiring from politics after his wife's lymphoma diagnosis in 2012. He was Deputy Attorney-General from March 2017 to January 2023, handling high-profile cases including the City Harvest Church appeal, the Chia Kee Chen murder case, the Gardens by the Bay murder case, and the Parti Liyani prosecutorial review. He was appointed Judge of the High Court in January 2023 and elevated to the Court of Appeal in October 2025. He is married to a partner at Ernst & Young and has one daughter.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with personal life details (wife at Ernst & Young, lymphoma diagnosis, 9 siblings, name spelling origin), high-profile prosecution cases (City Harvest, Chia Kee Chen, Gardens by the Bay murder, Roshdi Abdullah), political stances (377A repeal support, NMP-Cabinet proposal), and colleague relationship with Josephine Teo from Bishan-Toa Payoh GRC. Wife and daughter names not publicly disclosed.
